modes.Standard:
Standard (Balanced Approach): 
- Objective: Balance scientific validity, patient safety, and recruitment feasibility.
- Typical profile: Clinically justified thresholds; excludes known high-risk groups; allows common comorbidities with controls.
modes.Broader:
Broader (Increased Generalizability): 
- Objective: Maximize real-world applicability and diversity, accepting minor increases in variability to reflect real-world patient mix.
- Typical profile: Fewer exclusions; wider ranges (age, labs); Fewer lines of criteria.
modes.Stringent:
Stringent (Maximized Internal Validity): 
- Objective: Reduce confounding and maximize signal detection for confirmatory efficacy, prioritizing internal validity over recruitment speed or generalizability.
- Typical profile: Narrower disease definitions/staging; stricter lab/vital thresholds. More lines of criteria.
